title: Fire safety — what to do when you discover a fire
source: MR04 Fire Safety Policy and Procedure
scopeNote: Follows the policy's 'on discovering a fire' sequence. 999 is called at the moment of discovery, alongside the alarm — not last at the assembly point.

steps.label: Raise the alarm and call 999
steps.caption: The moment you find a fire, set off the alarm and call 999.

steps.label: Don't tackle large fires
steps.caption: Only use an extinguisher on a small fire if you are trained and it is safe.

steps.label: Leave by the nearest exit
steps.caption: Use your nearest escape route and never use the lift.

steps.label: Follow each person's PEEP
steps.caption: People who need help have a Personal Emergency Evacuation Plan — follow it.

steps.label: Close doors behind you
steps.caption: Closing doors slows the spread of fire and smoke.

steps.label: Go to the assembly point
steps.caption: Gather at the assembly point so everyone can be accounted for.

steps.label: Check everyone is out
steps.caption: The person in charge checks who is out and who is still inside.

endFrame: Out fast, stay out, and account for everyone.
